description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses node:latest as the default image and python:latest for the deploy jobs, defines the stages build and deploy, and sets the variables AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=us-east-1 and BUCKET_NAME=jaam.app. In the build stage add a build_frontend job that caches the node_modules directory, runs "yarn install --pure-lockfile" then "yarn build", and publishes the build/ folder as an artifact. In the deploy stage add a deploys3 job that uses python:latest, runs "pip install awscli" in before_script, depends on build_frontend, sets the environment name to ${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG} with an on_stop reference to clean_s3, uploads the build directory to s3://${BUCKET_NAME} recursively with "aws s3 cp build s3://${BUCKET_NAME} --recursive", and provides the S3 website URL as the environment URL. Also add a clean_s3 manual job that uses python:latest, runs "pip install awscli" then "aws s3 rm s3://${BUCKET_NAME}/${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG} --recursive", stops the environment, and is triggered only manually.
